request.description:
Returns public team section data and team members for the `Meet Our Team` area.

Auth: none.
Method: GET
Success: 200 OK

Response highlights:
- `data.section.badge`: Small section label.
- `data.section.title`: Main heading.
- `data.section.subtitle`: Supporting copy.
- `data.members`: Team member cards with name, role, and image URL.

Returns frontend-ready screen data for the `Meet Our Team` section shown in the design.

Auth: none.
Method: GET
Success: 200 OK

Response highlights:
- `data.screen.badge`: Small pill label.
- `data.screen.title`: Main screen title.
- `data.screen.subtitle`: Supporting description.
- `data.screen.layout`: Suggested responsive grid settings.
- `data.screen.cards`: Pre-mapped UI cards with overlay metadata.

Returns frontend-ready data for the public `Get in Touch` page.

Auth: none.
Method: GET
Success: 200 OK

Response highlights:
- `data.screen.title`: Main heading.
- `data.screen.form.fields`: Input/select definitions for the form.
- `data.screen.contactMethods`: Phone, fax, and email blocks.
- `data.screen.form.submitAction`: Public submit API metadata.

Submits a public contact enquiry.

Auth: none.
Method: POST
Content-Type: application/json
Success: 201 Created

}

request.description:
Returns all team members for admin management.

Auth: Bearer token required and user must be an admin.
Method: GET
Success: 200 OK

Creates a new team member.

Auth: Bearer token required and user must be an admin.
Method: POST
Content-Type: form-data or JSON
Success: 201 Created

Accepted fields:
- `name` required
- `role` required
- `bio` optional
- `imageUrl` optional when sending JSON
- `file` or `image` or `photo` optional when uploading multipart image
- `order` optional
- `isPublished` optional

formdata.value: Leads strategy and partnerships for the training platform.

Success: 200 OK

formdata.value: Updated profile copy for the public team section.

request.description:
Returns all public contact form submissions for admins.

Success: 200 OK

Optional query params:

item.name: Get Admin Contact Message By Id

request.description:
Returns one public contact message for admins.

Auth: Bearer token required and must be an admin.
Method: GET
Path param: `contactMessageId`
Success: 200 OK

Updates a public contact message status.

Auth: Bearer token required and must be an admin.

Content-Type: application/json

Returns mobile/web-ready catalog screen data for authenticated users.

Use this endpoint to render the course listing UI without reshaping the generic course list response.

Auth: Bearer token required.
Method: GET
Success: 200 OK

Response highlights:
- `data.screen.title`: Screen title.
- `data.screen.subtitle`: Screen subtitle.
- `data.screen.filters`: Active search/status filters.
- `data.screen.cards`: Pre-mapped course cards for the frontend.
- `data.screen.pagination`: Pagination metadata.

Returns the frontend-ready course detail screen used by the normal course details page.

Auth: Bearer token required.
Method: GET
Path param: `courseSlug`
Success: 200 OK

Use this for the screen with:
- hero image/gallery
- qualification, location, entry requirements, duration, price
- `Book Now` CTA
- related courses cards

Checks whether the selected course needs a special modal before booking.

Auth: Bearer token required.
Method: GET
Path param: `courseSlug`
Success: 200 OK

Normal course behaviour:
- `data.modal` is usually `null`.
- The frontend can go directly to booking creation / checkout.

AM2-related courses still return the eligibility modal payload.

Returns the first registration screen for the selected course.

Auth: Bearer token required.
Method: GET
Path param: `courseSlug`
Success: 200 OK

Response highlights:
- `data.screen.courseContext`: Course metadata used across the form journey.
- `data.screen.steps`: Registration progress tracker.
- `data.screen.sections`: Form sections and fields for candidate details.
- `data.screen.submission`: Payload template for `POST /api/bookings`.

After this first save, use the returned `booking.id` and `booking.registration.endpoints.*` URLs for the remaining registration steps.

Returns the assessment and registration details step in the registration journey.

Auth: Bearer token required.
Method: GET
Path param: `courseSlug`
Success: 200 OK

Response highlights:
- `data.screen.sections[0].fields`: Radio/text fields for assessment data.
- `data.screen.navigation.previous`: Previous step URL.
- `data.screen.navigation.next`: Next step URL.
- `data.screen.submission.apiUrlTemplate`: `POST /api/bookings/{bookingId}/registration/assessment`.
- `data.screen.submission.payloadTemplate`: Shape expected by the assessment save endpoint.

Returns the current employer details step for the course registration flow.

Auth: Bearer token required.
Method: GET
Path param: `courseSlug`
Success: 200 OK

Response highlights:
- Address/contact field set for employer details.
- Step navigation metadata.
- `data.screen.submission.apiUrlTemplate`: `POST /api/bookings/{bookingId}/registration/employer`.
- Submission payload template for saving the active booking employer step.

Returns the training provider / certificate issuer step for the course registration flow.

Auth: Bearer token required.
Method: GET
Path param: `courseSlug`
Success: 200 OK

Use this endpoint to render the provider/college details screen after employer details are collected.

Save with `POST /api/bookings/{bookingId}/registration/training`.

Returns the privacy notice and final confirmation step for the course registration flow.

Auth: Bearer token required.
Method: GET
Path param: `courseSlug`
Success: 200 OK

Response highlights:
- Privacy notice content blocks.
- Mandatory confirmation checkbox.
- `data.screen.submission.apiUrlTemplate`: `POST /api/bookings/{bookingId}/registration/privacy`.
- Submission payload template containing `privacyConfirmation`.

This is the final registration-form screen before the booking continues into later booking flow actions.

Creates a booking for a normal course after the user presses `Book Now`.

Auth: Bearer token required.
Method: POST
Success: 201 Created or 200 OK when an existing pending booking is reused.

This is the first write API for the normal course flow. After success:
- store `data.booking.id` as `{{bookingId}}`
- open `GET /api/bookings/{{bookingId}}/checkout/details`

Note: `eligibilityCheck` is only needed for AM2-style flows, not the normal course pages shown in the screenshots.

item.name: Normal Course - Get Booking By Id

request.description:
Returns the first normal-course checkout screen shown after booking creation.

Auth: Bearer token required.
Method: GET
Path param: `bookingId`
Success: 200 OK

This matches the personal-details page in the screenshot.

Continue action:
- `PATCH /api/bookings/{{bookingId}}/details`
- next screen: `GET /api/bookings/{{bookingId}}/checkout/payment`

Returns the payment page for the normal course checkout flow.

Auth: Bearer token required.
Method: GET
Path param: `bookingId`
Success: 200 OK

This screen exposes Stripe action URLs for preparing the payment intent, completing payment, and polling payment status.

Saves the personal details from the first normal-course checkout screen.

Auth: Bearer token required.
Method: PATCH
Path param: `bookingId`
Success: 200 OK

{

request.description:
Prepares the Stripe payment intent for the normal-course payment screen.

Auth: Bearer token required.
Method: POST
Path param: `bookingId`
Success: 200 OK

Checks the latest payment status for the normal-course booking and returns confirmation data.

Auth: Bearer token required.
Method: GET
Path param: `bookingId`
Success: 200 OK

Completes booking payment.

Auth: Bearer token required.
Method: POST
Path param: `bookingId`
Success: 200 OK

Two supported ways to use this endpoint:
- Frontend flow: send `paymentIntentId` after Stripe.js confirms the PaymentIntent.
- Postman/test flow: send both `paymentIntentId` and a Stripe test `paymentMethodId` such as `pm_card_visa` so the backend can confirm the PaymentIntent server-side.

Returns the final confirmation page after the payment flow completes.

Auth: Bearer token required.
Method: GET
Path param: `bookingId`
Success: 200 OK

Saves the final privacy confirmation step from the NET registration flow.

Auth: Bearer token required.
Method: POST
Path param: `bookingId`
Success: 200 OK

Returns random mock registration data shaped for the NET registration flow, with optional course-aware values when a valid `courseId` is provided.

Auth: none.
Method: GET
Query param: `courseId`
Success: 200 OK

Response highlights:
- `data.mockRegistration.personalDetails`
- `data.mockRegistration.assessmentDetails`
- `data.mockRegistration.employerDetails`
- `data.mockRegistration.trainingProviderDetails`
- `data.pdfCoverage`

Returns the AM2 checklist flow definition for a given course, including course details, checklist sections/items, flow steps, and a coverage report comparing the backend flow with the provided AM2 checklist screens.

Auth: none.
Method: GET
Query param: `courseId`
Success: 200 OK

Returns the AM2E checklist flow definition for a given course. Use this when the user selects an eligible qualification and `Before 3rd September 2023` in the NVQ registration date step.

Auth: none.
Method: GET
Query params: `courseId`, `questionId`, `answerId`
- `questionId` must be `nvq-registration-date`
- `answerId` must be `before-3rd-september-2023`
Success: 200 OK

item.name: AM2E V1 Checklist Flow By Course

request.description:
Returns the AM2E V1 checklist flow definition for a given course. Use this when the user selects an eligible qualification and `After September 2023` in the NVQ registration date step.

Auth: none.
Method: GET
Query params: `courseId`, `questionId`, `answerId`
- `questionId` must be `nvq-registration-date`
- `answerId` must be `after-september-2023`
Success: 200 OK

Returns the first booking-flow page after the user has completed the initial course booking / eligibility journey.

This is the first screen shown for the booking flow in the screenshots: `Upload Full Certificate`.

Auth: Bearer token required.
Method: GET
Path param: `bookingId`
Success: 200 OK

Important note:
- This screen is the first page of the later AM2 flow.
- The earlier eligibility modal is loaded from `GET /api/courses/{{courseSlug}}/book-now`.
- After a booking exists, this endpoint is used to render the new documents screen for that booking.

Response highlights:
- `data.screen.steps`: Progress stepper (`Documents`, `Checklist`, `Signatures`, etc.).
- `data.screen.title`: Usually `Upload Full Certificate`.
- `data.screen.requirements`: Upload cards shown on the page.
- `data.screen.actions.continue`: Next API for the checklist summary screen.

Uploads a document for the booking documents screen.

Auth: Bearer token required.
Method: POST
Content-Type: multipart/form-data
Path param: `bookingId`
Success: 200 OK

Used from the `Upload Full Certificate` page to upload or re-upload one of the required files.

Form fields:
- `documentType`: Internal document type key.
- `documentLabel`: Human-readable label shown in the UI.
- `file`: Uploaded file binary.

formdata.value: Learner History Report or Walled Garden Report (City & Guilds)

request.description:
Returns the checklist summary page shown after the documents page.

Auth: Bearer token required.
Method: GET
Path param: `bookingId`
Success: 200 OK

This matches the screenshot with:
- `AM2E Checklist`
- `Open Full Checklist`
- `Continue`

Response highlights:
- `data.screen.overallCompletion`: Summary percentage.
- `data.screen.actions.openFullChecklist`: Opens the full checklist API.
- `data.screen.actions.continue`: Goes to the signatures screen when completion reaches 100%.

Returns the full AM2 checklist page for a specific section.

Auth: Bearer token required.
Method: GET
Path param: `bookingId`
Query param: `section`
Success: 200 OK

This endpoint drives the section tabs shown in the screenshots:
- `A1`
- `A2-A5`
- `B`
- `C`
- `D`
- `E`

Examples:
- `section=A1`
- `section=A2-A5`
- `section=B`
- `section=C`
- `section=D`
- `section=E`

Response highlights:
- `data.screen.sections`: Tab/section summary list.
- `data.screen.activeSection`: Currently selected section content.
- `data.screen.actions.saveDraft`: PATCH endpoint for saving answers.
- `data.screen.actions.nextSection`: Next section API or final submit action.

Saves checklist answers for one checklist section.

Auth: Bearer token required.
Method: PATCH
Content-Type: application/json
Path param: `bookingId`
Success: 200 OK

Use this from the full checklist page when the user presses `Save Draft` or completes a section.

Request body:
- `section`: Section key such as `A1`, `A2-A5`, `B`, `C`, `D`, or `E`.
- `responses`: Array of checklist item answers.
- `responses[].itemId`: Checklist item identifier.
- `responses[].knowledgeLevel`: `adequate`, `limited`, or `unsure`.
- `responses[].experienceLevel`: `adequate`, `limited`, or `unsure`.

Returns the signatures page shown after the checklist is complete.

Auth: Bearer token required.
Method: GET
Path param: `bookingId`
Success: 200 OK

This matches the screenshot with:
- candidate signature status
- training provider signature status
- `Submit Signature`
- `Ask for signed`

When both signatures are complete, continue to the new submit step `GET /api/bookings/{{bookingId}}/flow/submit`.

Submits the candidate signature from the signatures modal.

Auth: Bearer token required.
Method: POST
Path param: `bookingId`
Success: 200 OK

Sends the training provider signature request email.

Auth: Bearer token required.
Method: POST
Path param: `bookingId`
Success: 200 OK

This endpoint accepts the email modal fields and sends a secure link for the training provider to add their signature.

body.raw:
{
  "trainingProviderEmail": "provider@example.com",
  "trainingProviderName": "Daniel Reed",
  "subject": "Please add your signature",
  "message": "Hello,

I have completed my registration form and require your signature as my training provider to finalize the process.

Please review my details and provide your signature.

Thank you."
}

request.description:
Returns the submit step after documents, checklist, and both signatures are complete.

Auth: Bearer token required.
Method: GET
Path param: `bookingId`
Success: 200 OK

Submits the completed AM2 flow for admin review.

Auth: Bearer token required.
Method: POST
Path param: `bookingId`
Success: 200 OK

Returns the admin review screen for the AM2 flow.

Auth: Bearer token required.
Method: GET
Path param: `bookingId`
Success: 200 OK

Possible states:
- `submitted` -> Application Submitted / Pending Review
- `under_review` -> Under Review
- `approved` -> Application Approved
- `rejected` -> Application Rejected

Marks the AM2 submission as under review.

Auth: Bearer token required and must be an admin.
Method: PATCH
Path param: `bookingId`
Success: 200 OK

Approves the submitted AM2 booking so the user can continue from review to payment.

Auth: Bearer token required and must be an admin.
Method: PATCH
Path param: `bookingId`
Success: 200 OK

You can also set `applicationStatus` to `under_review` or `rejected` for the other review states.

Public endpoint opened from the training provider email link. No auth required.

Method: GET
Path param: `trainingProviderSignatureToken`
Success: 200 OK

Public endpoint used by the training provider link to submit the provider signature. No auth required.

Method: POST
Path param: `trainingProviderSignatureToken`
Success: 200 OK

Creates or reuses a Stripe PaymentIntent for the booking. Call this before confirming payment on the frontend with Stripe.js.

Auth: Bearer token required.
Method: POST
Path param: `bookingId`
Success: 200 OK

Polls Stripe payment status for a booking and syncs the backend booking state.

Auth: Bearer token required.
Method: GET
Path param: `bookingId`
Success: 200 OK

Completes booking payment.

Auth: Bearer token required.
Method: POST
Path param: `bookingId`
Success: 200 OK

Two supported ways to use this endpoint:
- Frontend flow: send `paymentIntentId` after Stripe.js confirms the PaymentIntent.
- Postman/test flow: send both `paymentIntentId` and a Stripe test `paymentMethodId` such as `pm_card_visa` so the backend can confirm the PaymentIntent server-side.

Webhook endpoint for Stripe events such as `payment_intent.succeeded` and `payment_intent.payment_failed`. In production this should be called by Stripe, not manually.

Method: POST

Returns frontend-ready support page data for the logged-in user.

Auth: Bearer token required.
Method: GET
Success: 200 OK

Response highlights:
- `data.screen.title`: Page title.
- `data.screen.actions.newTicket`: API metadata for opening the new ticket modal.
- `data.screen.sections.myTickets.tickets`: Ticket cards for the support page.
- `data.screen.form.fields`: Form metadata for the new ticket modal.

Auth: Bearer token required.
Method: GET
Success: 200 OK

Optional query params:
- `status`: `new`, `in_progress`, `resolved`
- `priority`: `low`, `medium`, `high`
- `category`: `bookings`, `payment`, `documents`, `checklist`, `signature`, `technical`, `general`
- `search`: free text

exec: const firstNotification = response && response.data && response.data.notifications && response.data.notifications[0];

request.description:
Returns notifications for the authenticated user or admin.

Success: 200 OK

This endpoint is populated automatically when:
- a candidate submits a booking for review -> admins get a notification
- an admin approves a booking -> the candidate gets a notification

Success: 200 OK
